name: Strait of Hormuz
summary: Day 105 / 2026-06-12. **THE STRAIT BECAME THE CENTRAL BARGAINING CHIP — A DEAL WOULD REOPEN IT IN ~30 DAYS, BUT IRAN INTENDS TO KEEP 'CONTROL' AND THE BLOCKADE STAYS UNTIL SIGNING.** Reported deal terms have Hormuz reopening to pre-war volumes within 30 days and the US lifting its naval blockade. Two caveats define the trade: (1) Araghchi says Iran will 'maintain control' of the Strait even under a deal — reopening restores FLOW but not necessarily the pre-war freedom-of-navigation regime, so the structural floor under oil may be higher than a clean reopening implies; (2) Trump says the blockade stays 'in full force until finalized,' so the ~5%-of-normal-flow paralysis and the lethal enforcement campaign continue until a signature. The market priced the reopening hope hard: Brent -4.1% to $86.71 (WTI below $85), the war premium finally deflating DOWN on de-escalation after refusing to bid UP through the kinetic escalation. Physical (VLCC rates, war-risk cover ~7-10% of hull) has NOT unwound — paper-oil drops on hope, physical waits for the signature. Tanker equities (Frontline +9.8%) priced reopening as VOLUME-bullish (idled-fleet re-employment).

current.status: BLOCKADE STAND-DOWN ORDERED, REOPENING NOT YET OCCURRING (2026-06-18): US blockade-removal order CONFIRMED (14 Jun), but independent AIS shows traffic UNCHANGED -- ~18 vessels, Iranian-domestic-flag only, foreign-flag absent, ~95% below pre-war (Argus/GEF). Strait physically closed (Day 108). Foreign-flag transit awaits Friday signing + demining (~6mo for full).

active_triggers.condition: Hormuz reopens to all traffic (even partially)
active_triggers.note_2026-06-06: STAYS WATCHING. No reopening on Day ~99 — transits still ~10/day vs ~95 normal. The 5-6 June overnight exchange (4 drones toward Hormuz, all intercepted; US Goruk/Qeshm strike) and Rezaei's sovereignty/Oman/toll rhetoric are NOT closure or reopening acts; throughput unchanged. No move.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS WATCHING. No reopening — the Strait became the locus of a US-Iran kinetic exchange (Apache downed over Hormuz 8 Jun; US strikes on Iranian coastal air-defense near the Strait 9 Jun; Iran's 10-June Gulf strikes launched from the region). Traffic ~-95%; IEA 'largest supply disruption in history.' Structure unchanged.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-12: STAYS WATCHING — but for the first time the vector points TOWARD reopening (deal terms: pre-war volumes within 30 days). NOT fired: unsigned, and Araghchi says Iran retains 'control' even post-deal, so a clean reopening is not assured. Fires only on operationally-confirmed restored transit.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-13: STAYS WATCHING. A signed deal would reopen 'immediately without tolls' (~30 days to pre-war volumes) — but it is unsigned, Iran retains a 'service fee' role, and demining/restart delays actual normalization. Fires only on operationally-confirmed restored transit.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-16: 2026-06-16: Reopening AGREED on paper (within 30d) but NOT operational — strait still closed Day 107, zero transit surge. STAYS watching until actual transits resume.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-17: 2026-06-17: MOVED watching->active. The US naval blockade — the mechanism that closed Hormuz — is being removed by CONFIRMED order of two governments (Trump's authorisation + Iran's confirmation), and first transits are reported. 'Even partially' threshold met by the confirmed blockade removal. This is a confirmed de-escalation ACTION, not rhetoric.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-18: 2026-06-18: WALKED BACK active->watching. Yesterday's activation rested partly on a single-source transit report; independent AIS now shows traffic UNCHANGED (foreign-flag absent, ~95% below pre-war). The 48h reconfirm flagged in the 17 Jun verification FAILED. The blockade-removal ORDER stays confirmed, but 'reopens even partially' requires actual traffic, which is not occurring. Disciplined self-correction -- revert to watching until AIS shows foreign-flag transit resumption.

active_triggers.condition: US attempts naval escort or forced opening of Hormuz

War risk insurance for Gulf transits breaks below 0.5% hull value (normalization threshold) — REFRAMED 2026-05-06: 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% within 10 sessions' is now the diplomatic-track-durability test under the Trump pause framework
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS WATCHING — and a correction-grade RE-BASE: Gulf war-risk cover is now ~4% of hull value per 7-day transit (The National, 3 Jun), NOT the ~1% carried in recent briefs. Far from the 0.5% normalization threshold; cover RISING, not normalizing. The US directed the DFC to provide a political-risk reinsurance facility up to $40bn to keep Hormuz shipping moving.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-16: 2026-06-16: Premiums (~0.5% of cargo, up from ~0.2%) now signalled to DECLINE and VLCC rates easing off the ~$50K/day Israel-strike spike — the unwind is BEGINNING from the physical side, but not yet below 0.5% normalized and strait still closed. STAYS watching; convergence underway.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-17: 2026-06-17: Blockade lift should drive sharp insurance/VLCC normalization, but no confirmed sub-0.5% level yet. STAYS watching; unwind accelerating.

active_triggers.condition: Iran formally reopens Hormuz to all traffic within 14-21 days of May 5 pause (by 2026-05-26) — would collapse tanker spot rates -15-25%
active_triggers.note_2026-06-02: EXPIRED UNTRIPPED. The deadline (2026-05-26) passed with no reopening, and the vector has since INVERTED: on 1 June Iran threatened to 'completely close' Hormuz (CLAIMED escalation from selective control). Operationally the strait remains at ~4 ships/day (CNN '94 days of paralysis'). Status moved `watching` -> `resolved` (expired untripped) — a confirmed non-event on the reopening side; the live risk is fuller closure, not reopening.

active_triggers.condition: Second commercial vessel attack within 7 days inside Qatari EEZ following Sunday May 10 bulk carrier strike — confirms IRGC graduated-pressure-on-mediator-coastline pattern
Kharg Island slick re-appears on subsequent satellite passes (Copernicus Sentinel) within 14 days — confirms Iran storage-overflow / regime-dumping hypothesis vs European-tanker-waste counter-claim
active_triggers.activation_note: Second slick detected May 11-12 (~12-20 sq km, smaller but persistent; UN warned 'major disaster' risk). Re-appearance has now occurred. Hypothesis tilt toward storage-overflow / regime-dumping has strengthened modestly; CAUSE remains formally contested (Iran maintains European tanker waste claim).
active_triggers.note_2026-06-11: STAYS WATCHING. Trump's 11 Jun Truth Social Kharg-seizure post is the strongest rhetorical signal yet but was walked back same-cycle ('not sure America has the stomach') with NO force-posture/amphibious-group reporting. Window closes 2026-06-15; watch daily through Sunday.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-12: STAYS WATCHING — probability fallen with the cancelled strikes and the deal track. Window closes 15 Jun.

active_triggers.condition: Second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 Hodeidah-area UKMTO advisory — would collapse Houthi non-action read
active_triggers.expiry_note: Day 7 of 7 closes Monday May 18 with no second incident confirmed. UKMTO recent-incidents page shows no new advisory through Monday morning IST; MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement. **Houthi non-action during a publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase** (NYT preparation report May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu call May 17, Saudi drone interception May 17, UAE Barakah drone strike May 17) **is the cleanest behavioural divergence from the March-April pattern** when Houthi attacks compounded with US escalation rhetoric. The May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident now reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of a new pattern. Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification of Trump posts — coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise. The investor read: Iran signalling escalation control, not escalation.

active_triggers.condition: US ground operation against Kharg Island launched within 30 days of NYT preparation report (by 2026-06-15)
active_triggers.note: NYT (Friday May 15 / Saturday May 16, citing two Middle Eastern officials) REPORTED ground forces to seize Kharg Island as one of two options for renewed US strikes. Kharg handles up to 90% of Iranian oil exports. Iran has REPORTEDLY moved air defenses to Kharg in recent weeks. If operation launches, Brent gaps +$8-15 toward $130-145 per markets-dossier reversibility map; AWRP doubles; VLCC rates re-rate.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS WATCHING. No Kharg ground operation; the US response to the Apache downing was air/missile 'warning-shot' strikes on ~20 Iranian coastal air-defense sites near the Strait, not a ground op.

active_triggers.condition: Iran-permitted Chinese transit exceeds 50 vessels/week sustained 2 weeks (the selective-transit instrument becomes a structural rather than diplomatic-event signal)
active_triggers.note: Day 3 of 14 measurement window. No incremental vessel-count update published over the weekend. If sustained, Iran's selective Hormuz instrument is operationally durable, not a one-week diplomatic gesture.

active_triggers.condition: Hormuz remains under Iranian selective control even within ceasefire framework — ACTIVE 2026-04-08: Iran's 'controlled passage via coordination with armed forces' framing in the ceasefire text formali
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS active. The 7-10 June US-Iran kinetic exchange occurred AROUND the Strait (Apache downing, US coastal-air-defense strikes, Iran's Gulf strikes launched from the region) but did not change the selective-control structure; ISW (6 Jun) reads Iran's 'protection racket' continuing.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-11: STAYS active. The 10 Jun 'complete closure' declaration supersedes the selective-control framing as POLICY (no nationality exceptions), but enforcement is unconfirmed and CENTCOM says transits continue -- the operative regime on the water is unchanged. Stays active pending operational confirmation of the closure.

active_triggers.condition: Physical-futures gap closes below $10/bbl (Dated Brent converges to Brent futures)

Operationally-confirmed ENFORCEMENT of the 10 Jun complete-closure declaration -- Iran actually fires on / disables a transiting NEUTRAL vessel, verified by UKMTO or an independent maritime tracker (not an IRGC claim) -- converts the declaration from policy to action
active_triggers.note: As of 11 Jun the closure is a DECLARATION; the only enforcement is the IRGC's uncorroborated 2-tanker claim, which CENTCOM contradicts. This trigger fires only on operationally-verified enforcement against a neutral hull -- the event that would convert the dead war-premium into a violent Brent gap toward $110+ and double AWRP. The market is positioned for choreography; this is the un-priced tail.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-12: STAYS WATCHING. No new operationally-confirmed strike on a neutral hull this window; the blockade stays 'in full force until finalized' but the deal track lowers near-term enforcement-escalation risk.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-16: 2026-06-16: No new enforcement action; ceasefire holding. De-escalation, no fresh closure-enforcement signal.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-17: 2026-06-17: Superseded — blockade now lifting, not enforcing closure.

top_edges.context: 2026-06-11: Iran's top joint military command formally declared 'complete closure' of Hormuz (no nationality exceptions) -- ends the selective-control regime as POLICY; enforcement CLAIMED/contested (CENTCOM says transits continue).

2026-06-10: The Strait became the locus of the new US-Iran exchange — Iran downed a US Apache over Hormuz (8 Jun), US struck Iranian coastal air-defense/radar/ground-control near the Strait (9 Jun, 'warning shot'). The US also directed the DFC to provide a political-risk reinsurance facility up to $40bn to keep Hormuz shipping moving. CONFIRMED action; operational structure unchanged.
2026-06-13: A signed deal reopens Hormuz but normalization is slow (demining/restart) and Iran keeps a ~$1/bbl service fee — the oil downside is capped above the pre-war baseline.
2026-06-12: Reopening read as VOLUME-bullish for tankers (Frontline +9.8%) — idled-fleet re-employment; physical rates still elevated until signing.

recent_signals.headline: REOPENING NOT MATERIALISING ON THE WATER. Independent AIS audits (Argus 'Hormuz tanker traffic unchanged after US-Iran deal'; GEF: ~18 vessels, Iranian-domestic-flag only, foreign-flag absent, ~95% below pre-war) contradict the 17 Jun single-source claim that tankers had begun transiting. Shipowners are waiting for Friday's signing + details before attempting transits. The blockade-removal ORDER remains confirmed, but the physical reopening is ORDERED, NOT OCCURRING. CONFIRMED (operational AIS).

PARTIAL REOPENING UNDERWAY. Trump authorised the immediate removal of the US naval blockade (CONFIRMED — Truth Social/PBS/CBS) and Iran confirmed the blockade is lifting; specialised maritime reporting says Iranian tankers have begun transiting Hormuz (REPORTED, single-source). This is the first operational reopening signal of the war — distinct from the paper framework. Sustained-volume confirmation (>30 vessels/day x3) and independent AIS still pending; full normalization lags on ~6-month demining. CONFIRMED (blockade-removal order) / REPORTED (actual transit).

STILL CLOSED, Day 107 — despite the framework. The 14 Jun deal commits to reopening within ~30 days 'under Iranian arrangements' and lifting the US blockade within ~30 days, but operational trackers show no transits resumed. Pentagon planners assess clearing 20+ naval mines at up to ~6 months even at accelerated pace; the 107-day standoff cut crude transits ~95% and LNG ~99%. Political agreement != physical reopening. CONFIRMED still-closed (trackers) / REPORTED timelines.

DEAL WOULD REOPEN HORMUZ 'IMMEDIATELY WITHOUT TOLLS' (~30 DAYS TO PRE-WAR VOLUMES) — BUT IRAN KEEPS A 'SERVICE FEE' (~$1/BBL) AND NORMALIZATION IS SLOW (DEMINING). Araghchi: international law bars 'tolls' but Iran collects 'service fees' (the Persian Gulf Strait Authority's ~$1/bbl since 5 May), with compensation to Iran in the plan; Trump called the fee 'a beautiful thing.' A subsequent deal phase is DEMINING the waterway; analysts stress idled fields must restart and damaged facilities be repaired before flows normalize. So a signed deal ends the acute crisis but leaves a structurally HIGHER chokepoint cost-floor and a multi-week physical normalization — capping the oil downside.
recent_signals.verification: REPORTED (reopening terms); CONFIRMED (service-fee + demining caveats)

recent_signals.headline: DEAL WOULD REOPEN HORMUZ IN ~30 DAYS — BUT IRAN INTENDS TO RETAIN 'CONTROL' EVEN POST-DEAL, AND THE BLOCKADE STAYS UNTIL SIGNING. Reported terms (CBS/CNBC): reopening to pre-war volumes within 30 days + US lifting the naval blockade. BUT Araghchi: 'Iran will maintain control over the Strait of Hormuz... the future of the Strait will not be the same as in the past.' And Trump: 'The Naval Blockade will remain in full force until this Transaction is finalized.' So a signed deal reopens FLOW but may NOT restore pre-war freedom-of-navigation; until signing, the ~5%-flow blockade persists. Oil priced the reopening hope hard: Brent -4.1% to $86.71.
recent_signals.verification: REPORTED (reopening term); CONFIRMED (Iran-retains-control + blockade-stays positions)

recent_signals.headline: FORMAL 'COMPLETE CLOSURE' DECLARED -- announcement CONFIRMED, operational delta CONTESTED. Iran's top joint military command announced Hormuz 'closed until further notice' for oil tankers AND commercial vessels, 'will be shot at,' NO nationality exceptions -- formally ending the selective/coordinated-passage regime (incl. the permitted Chinese transits the graph tracked). IRGC claimed 2 transiting tankers hit (CLAIMED -- no UKMTO/independent corroboration; UKMTO page 403). CENTCOM countered that commercial ships continue to transit (direct contradiction). Strait already ~5% of normal flow (Day ~104), so the declaration shifts the POLICY perimeter more than observed throughput. Aluminum recovered toward ~$3,600 on the formalization where gold did not move.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED (the declaration); CLAIMED (tanker hits); CONTESTED (actual throughput change)

recent_signals.headline: **THE STRAIT BECAME THE LOCUS OF A NEW US-IRAN KINETIC EXCHANGE — APACHE DOWNED OVER HORMUZ (8 JUN), US STRIKES ON IRANIAN COASTAL AIR-DEFENSE/RADAR/GROUND-CONTROL SITES NEAR THE STRAIT (9 JUN), IRAN'S 10-JUNE GULF STRIKES LAUNCHED FROM THE REGION — STRUCTURE UNCHANGED (~-95% TRAFFIC, IEA 'LARGEST SUPPLY DISRUPTION IN HISTORY'). CORRECTION: GULF WAR-RISK COVER IS NOW ~4% OF HULL VALUE PER 7-DAY TRANSIT (NOT THE ~1% CARRIED IN RECENT BRIEFS). NEW: US DIRECTED THE DFC TO PROVIDE A POLITICAL-RISK REINSURANCE FACILITY UP TO $40 BILLION TO KEEP HORMUZ SHIPPING MOVING.** Iran downed a US Apache over Hormuz (8 Jun); US struck Iranian coastal radar/air-defense/ground-control near the Strait (9 Jun); Iran's 10-June strikes launched from the region — but no new physical closure act. Western-allied traffic near-zero; the US-guided Oman-coast channel carried ~70 ships in 3 weeks to 1 Jun; ~20,000 seafarers stranded on ~2,000 vessels; ISW (6 Jun) reads Iran's 'protection racket' continuing (IRGC 'warning shots' near Larak Island). Iran ran the Strait and Israel axes SEPARATELY during the 7-8 June exchange (no Strait drones) until the Apache event re-fused them 8-9 June. INSURANCE RE-BASE (correction-grade, REPORTED): ~4% of hull value per 7-day transit (The National, 3 Jun). DFC $40bn reinsurance facility is new. No Hormuz trigger fires (structure unchanged).
sources: CENTCOM / US military (Apache downed over Hormuz 8 Jun; US strikes on Iranian coastal air-defense/radar/ground-control near the Strait 9 Jun)
IEA ('largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market')
ISW/Critical Threats (6 Jun: Iran 'protection racket'; IRGC 'warning shots' near Larak Island)
Independent Hormuz transit trackers (~-95% traffic; ~70 ships in 3 weeks via Oman-coast channel; ~20,000 seafarers on ~2,000 vessels)
The National (3 Jun: Gulf war-risk cover ~4% of hull value per 7-day transit — correction-grade re-base)
US DFC (Development Finance Corp political-risk reinsurance facility up to $40bn for Hormuz shipping)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED ACTION (Apache downing over Hormuz; US coastal-site strikes near the Strait; operational chokehold ~-95%, IEA characterization) / REPORTED (insurance ~4% re-base; DFC $40bn reinsurance facility; ISW 'protection racket'). No new physical closure act; no trigger moves to active.

recent_signals.headline: **OPERATIONALLY UNCHANGED ON DAY ~99 (~10 SHIPS/DAY VS ~95 NORMAL, EFFECTIVELY CLOSED) — 4 IRANIAN DRONES LAUNCHED TOWARD HORMUZ (ALL INTERCEPTED); REZAEI SOVEREIGNTY/OMAN/TOLL FRAMING IS RHETORIC, NO NEW CLOSURE ACT.** No operational change: ~10 ships/day vs ~95 pre-crisis; Maersk/CMA CGM/MSC/Hapag-Lloyd still suspended; ~405 vessels anchored; CENTCOM has escorted ~70 vessels cumulatively. In the 5-6 June overnight exchange Iran launched 4 one-way attack drones toward Hormuz — all 4 intercepted/downed by US — and the US struck Iranian coastal radar at Goruk + Qeshm in self-defence; NO new physical closure act, throughput unchanged. Rezaei reasserted the 'sovereignty + Oman joint-management + transit-tolls' framing = RHETORIC on top of an already-throttled strait. Brent fell ~2% to ~$93 over the window on weak China demand, not on any Hormuz throughput change. No Hormuz trigger fires on rhetoric or an intercepted drone launch.
sources: Independent Hormuz transit trackers / shipping maps (~10 ships/day vs ~95; ~405 anchored; ~70 CENTCOM-escorted cumulatively)
CENTCOM / US military (official) — 4 drones toward Hormuz intercepted; US self-defence strikes on Goruk + Qeshm coastal radar
IRGC / Rezaei — Hormuz sovereignty + Oman joint-management + transit-toll framing (Iran rhetoric, not an act)
recent_signals.verification: Operational chokehold persists (~10 ships/day, ~405 anchored, ~70 escorted) = CONFIRMED (independent trackers); 4 drones toward Hormuz all intercepted + US Goruk/Qeshm strike = CONFIRMED ACTION; Rezaei sovereignty/Oman/toll framing = RHETORIC (not an act, not elevated). No new physical closure act; no trigger moves to active.

recent_signals.headline: **OPERATIONALLY UNCHANGED (~5% OF PRE-WAR TRAFFIC) ON DAY 96 — BUT A US SELF-DEFENCE STRIKE LANDED ON QESHM ISLAND INSIDE THE STRAIT, AND IRAN CLAIMED THE US HIT AN OIL TANKER.** In the 3 June exchange CENTCOM struck an Iranian ground-control station on Qeshm Island in the strait (and reportedly Goruk); Iran/IRGC claimed the US hit an oil tanker and a 'communications tower south of Qeshm,' and that it coordinated 26 vessels through under 'intelligent control' (CLAIMED, discrepant with ~4-5/day trackers — NOT elevated). No confirmed change to the throughput regime — no full closure, no forced reopening; 'complete closure' stays RHETORIC. DHL still forecasts 4-6 months to normalisation. No Hormuz trigger fires on rhetoric or an intraday Brent tag.
sources: CENTCOM (Qeshm self-defence strike) — official military; IRGC/Tasnim (US hit oil tanker / 26 vessels claim) — Iran claim
2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis tracker / CNN shipping maps (~5% of normal); DHL normalisation forecast (4-6 months)
recent_signals.verification: US Qeshm strike = CONFIRMED ACTION; '~5% of pre-war traffic / no operational change' = CONFIRMED (independent trackers); Iran 'US hit oil tanker' + '26 vessels intelligent control' + 'complete closure' = CLAIM/RHETORIC (not elevated). No trigger to active.

recent_signals.headline: **IRAN THREATENED TO 'COMPLETELY CLOSE' HORMUZ (ESCALATION FROM SELECTIVE CONTROL) — BUT OPERATIONALLY UNCHANGED AT ~4 SHIPS/DAY; IRGC '24 VESSELS / INTELLIGENT CONTROL' CLAIM IS DISCREPANT.** Tied to the 1 June suspension of US talks, Tasnim vowed the 'resistance front' would 'completely close the Strait of Hormuz.' Per CNBC/IBTimes this is a THREAT, not an implemented closure ('no operational blockade confirmed as implemented'). CNN's 2 June piece ('94 days of paralysis') reports only ~4 vessels/day vs ~95 normal — effectively closed since 28 Feb, unchanged. The IRGC claimed 24 vessels passed under 'intelligent control' (CLAIMED Iran, discrepant with independent ~4/day — NOT elevated; Tehran projecting control ahead of threatening to remove it). The 1 June closure threat re-priced Brent +7.3% intraday to ~$97 then faded to ~$94.5 — risk-premium re-entry, not supply disruption (throughput unchanged). No reopening trigger fires; the 'complete closure' threat moves no trigger to active (rhetoric). Watch the independent transit count, not the IRGC's number.
sources: CNBC — 'Iran vows to completely block Strait of Hormuz' — independent
IBTimes UK — 'no operational blockade confirmed as implemented' (threat, not action) — independent
CNN (2 June) — '94 days of paralysis... handful of vessels vs ~95' — operational reporting — independent
straits.live (status: Closed, June 2026) — operational tracker — independent
IRGC / Tasnim — '24 vessels / intelligent control' — Iran claim (NOT elevated)
recent_signals.verification: Operational chokehold persists at ~4 ships/day = CONFIRMED (CNN + straits.live); 'complete closure' = RHETORIC/THREAT (CNBC/IBTimes, not implemented); IRGC '24 vessels / intelligent control' = CLAIMED Iran, discrepant with independent trackers, NOT elevated. No trigger moves to active.

recent_signals.headline: **NEW SUSPECTED MINE OFF OMAN (30 MAY); LIAN STAR INTERDICTION (6th VESSEL); US-TREASURY-VS-OMAN TOLL FRICTION.** Oman's Maritime Security Centre warned (30 May) of a floating object suspected to be a naval mine within Omani territorial waters, urging vessels to keep clear. The US disabled the Lian Star in the Gulf of Oman (6th blockade vessel); Hegseth said the blockade is 'very much still in place' and Hormuz must remain 'open, toll-free.' The US Treasury reportedly threatened to sanction Oman over Hormuz tolls — colliding with Iran's Oman-mediated toll ambition in the draft MOU. Hormuz remains mined and contested inside the ceasefire — the physical floor under oil even as the tail de-prices.
sources: Oman Maritime Security Centre advisory 30 May — operational/official
Iran International liveblog 30 May (Lian Star); Hegseth on record 30 May
Times of Israel liveblog 28 May (US Treasury-Oman tolls) — REPORTED, single headline
recent_signals.verification: Suspected-mine advisory = CONFIRMED ACTION (Oman MSC operational/official); Lian Star interdiction = REPORTED-to-CONFIRMED; Hegseth 'blockade still in place / toll-free' = CONFIRMED; US Treasury-Oman toll threat = REPORTED (single headline).

recent_signals.headline: **THE STRAIT IS STILL ACTIVELY CONTESTED INSIDE THE CEASEFIRE — US DOWNED 4 IRANIAN DRONES NEAR HORMUZ + STRUCK A GROUND-CONTROL STATION; NO REOPENING ON DAY 90; THE 'SECOND SEIZURE IN 7 DAYS' WATCH EXPIRED UNTRIPPED.** Early May 28 US forces shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones near Hormuz and struck an Iranian ground-control station near Bandar Abbas the Pentagon said was 'reportedly preparing to launch a fifth' drone (CONFIRMED ACTION — CENTCOM via CNN/NBC/Euronews/WaPo). The cleanest operational support yet for the 'physical risk persists' thesis: Iran is still contesting the strait with drones and mines even as the unsigned MOU calls for Hormuz 'open with no tolls' and Iran to clear the mines it laid. The IRGC's intercepted ballistic missile at Kuwait was the retaliation (A1); the maritime story is the drone/mine contest, NOT a fresh seizure — the 'second Iranian commercial-vessel seizure within 7 days' watch EXPIRED UNTRIPPED (last confirmed seizure remains May 14 Fujairah). UKMTO has logged 41+ incidents since late February; mines in/near the TSS persist; GNSS interference 'sporadic but reduced from March.' Hormuz remains effectively BLOCKED on Day 90 — no operational reopening. TRIGGERS: Hormuz-reopening/transit-volume stay watching; Iran-Oman toll-mechanism stays watching (no formal announcement).
sources: CENTCOM (4 drones downed near Hormuz + ground-control station struck near Bandar Abbas) — official military
UKMTO recent-incidents / Wikipedia '2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis' (41+ incidents; mines persist) — operational
UN News ('Uncertainty continues over safety in the Strait of Hormuz') — official
recent_signals.verification: US downing of 4 Iranian drones near Hormuz + strike on a ground-control station near Bandar Abbas = CONFIRMED ACTION (CENTCOM + multi-wire); Iran still contesting the strait with drones/mines inside the ceasefire = CONFIRMED; no operational reopening Day 90 = CONFIRMED (transits minimal); no new Iranian commercial-vessel seizure (watch expired untripped) = CONFIRMED non-event (negative search); UKMTO 41+ incidents / mines persist = REPORTED (operational aggregator).

recent_signals.headline: **THE US STRUCK IRGC FAST BOATS LAYING MINES *IN* HORMUZ (CONFIRMED ACTION, May 25) — mines still being laid even as the deal requires Iran to *clear* them. On Monday May 25 US forces struck two IRGC fast boats laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz + a Bandar Abbas SAM site that CENTCOM said was targeting US aircraft (CONFIRMED ACTION — CENTCOM/Hawkins 'self-defense,' 'restraint'; CNN/NBC/Al Jazeera/NPR). The targeting is the intelligence: it is the cleanest confirmation the physical risk inside the strait remains active even inside the ceasefire — validating Croft's 'fine print / insurers and shippers won't rush back if Iran continues to have the final say on passage' thesis. The deal is 'largely negotiated' (Trump): Hormuz reopened 'with no tolls' + Iran clears its mines, US lifts the blockade + sanctions waivers — but Hormuz control/status is one of three unresolved sticking points and Deputy FM Bagheri Kani said 'the two sides had not yet agreed on lifting the blockade.'** (1) **US strike on IRGC mine-laying boats in Hormuz (CONFIRMED ACTION)**: Iran was still laying mines in the strait the deal would require it to clear. (2) **Quad Foreign Ministers, New Delhi, May 26 — anti-toll UNCLOS statement (CONFIRMED diplomatic action — US State Dept joint statement, ANI, WaPo)**: 'deep concern over the Iranian closure' of Hormuz; reiterated UNCLOS navigational rights; condemned attacks on commercial shipping; and 'opposed any future measures that are inconsistent with UNCLOS, including imposition of tolls.' India put its name to language that attributes the closure to Iran and explicitly opposes Hormuz tolls — directly relevant to the deal's unresolved 'Hormuz management/tolls' clause. (3) **Operationally unchanged**: transits remain minimal; no operational reopening; the strait has been effectively closed 89 days. **TRIGGER DECISIONS**: transit-volume and second-seizure triggers stay watching (no Iranian seizure — the maritime story is the US strike on IRGC boats); Iran-Oman permanent toll mechanism trigger stays watching (Fars 'management' = pre-positioning, not a formal announcement).
sources: CENTCOM spokesman Timothy Hawkins (via CNBC/NBC/WaPo) — official military
CNN / Al Jazeera / NPR May 25-26 (US strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats + Bandar Abbas SAM site)

recent_signals.headline: **MANAGEMENT-VS-NO-TOLLS CONTRADICTION SHARPENED + NEW 30-DAY HORMUZ / 60-DAY NUCLEAR SPLIT; EU HORMUZ-DISRUPTION SANCTIONS FRAMEWORK NOW CONFIRMED-ADOPTED (MAY 22) — BUT NO DESIGNATIONS YET. HORMUZ TRANSITS REMAIN MINIMAL — NO OPERATIONAL REOPENING, DAY 87.** (1) **US/Axios framing now 'open with no tolls, mines cleared' [CONFIRMED — Axios May 24, CNN May 25]**: during the window the strait would be 'open with no tolls,' Iran clears the mines it laid, US lifts the blockade in exchange. (2) **Iranian framing still 'management, not free passage' [CONFIRMED — Times of Israel May 23 liveblog / BOE Report (Fars), Tasnim via aggregation]**: Fars's 'Iran's management / not free passage as before the war' line stands uncorrected; Tasnim allocates '30 days for procedures related to the Strait of Hormuz and 60 days for nuclear talks' — a bifurcated timeline beginning only after the framework is 'formally agreed' (Baghaei). (3) **The contradiction is not resolving toward either pole — both sides keep publishing their own version [CONTESTED which is in the text]**; Rubio's May 21 red line: a tolling/selective-management mechanism makes a deal 'unfeasible.' (4) **EU Hormuz sanctions framework — UPGRADED TO CONFIRMED [Brussels Signal, Marine Insight, Express Tribune, Sofia Globe, News24]**: the EU Council formally adopted on May 22 a framework allowing travel bans / asset freezes on individuals/entities disrupting Strait of Hormuz shipping, delivering the April 21 Foreign Affairs Council political agreement. **NO specific names designated yet — enabling instrument; listings 'would follow at a later stage.'** Cross-current worth flagging: the EU is erecting a Hormuz-disruption sanctions architecture at the exact moment the US-Iran deal would reopen the strait — a potential transatlantic divergence if the deal lands. (The action is 3 days old; only the corroboration is new.) (5) **Operationally unchanged**: transits remain minimal; no operational reopening; the strait has been effectively closed 87 days. Iran-Oman permanent toll mechanism trigger Day 4 of 14 stays `watching` — Fars 'management' = pre-positioning, not a formal announcement.

recent_signals.headline: **HORMUZ STATUS UNCHANGED ON POSTPONEMENT — ~1-30 VESSELS/DAY VS 138 PRE-WAR; AWRP 4-6X PRE-CRISIS; IRGC QESHM ISLAND DEFENSE-SYSTEMS REPORTEDLY ACTIVATED LATE MONDAY (REPORTED single-source).** Brent retraced -1.6% to $110.32 on the postponement (tail-risk de-pricing) but the structural premium is intact: $110.32 is still 51% above the $73 pre-crisis baseline. **The postponement re-prices the TAIL, not the structural premium.** Baker Hughes call: full Hormuz reopen NOT until 2H26. Turkey FM Hakan Fidan publicly stated the immediate concern of US-Iran negotiations is keeping the Strait of Hormuz open, but Iran's nuclear program remains a central issue (CONFIRMED RHETORIC) — first clean Turkish public framing of the cycle. **AWRP holds at 3-8% of vessel value (some 10%), 'double-digit millions per trip'** still operative. **The structural war premium has migrated OUT of crude flat-price INTO ton-miles (Frontline +2.9%), LNG basis (Cheniere +2.4%), and defense backlog (Lockheed +2.4% on $879M F-35 contract)** — these don't reverse on a single postponement.

Intel dossier 2026-05-19 B2; Markets dossier C1, C4

recent_signals.headline: **STANCHART $130-145 PATH ACTIVATES MAY 18 — Brent 2-session sustain rule met** ($109.26 Friday close + $111.47 Monday intraday, range $110.13-$111.99). The May 12-added 'Brent breaks $108-110 sustained 2 sessions' trigger fires (brent-crude node). Cascade fires same day: inr-usd ₹96.29 record low (trigger fires Day 6 of 5-7); Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04%; energy-sector XLE +2.4%; marine-war-risk-insurance edge weight raised to 10.0. **HOUTHI RED SEA DAY 7 OF 7 NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW CLOSES UNTRIPPED** — May 11 Hodeidah incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event'; no second incident through Monday IST per UKMTO. Houthi non-action during a publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase (NYT preparation May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu call May 17, Saudi/UAE drone events May 17) is the cleanest behavioural divergence from the March-April pattern. **NEW WEEKEND KINETIC EVENTS**: (i) UAE Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant — May 17 drone strike on perimeter generator (3 drones from western border; 2 intercepted; 1 caused fire outside inner perimeter); IAEA expressed 'grave concern,' radiation normal, all units operating — first nuclear-infrastructure-adjacent kinetic of cycle; (ii) Saudi defense ministry — May 17 3 drones from Iraqi airspace intercepted over Saudi airspace; Brig Turki al-Maliki: 'right to respond at the appropriate time and place'; UAE MoFA formal condemnation May 18 — first confirmed Saudi territorial breach of cycle; Iraqi militia (PMU/Kata'ib Hezbollah/Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq) most likely vectors by capability and prior pattern; no group claimed responsibility, consistent with deniable harassment. **Hormuz physical state unchanged**: ~30 vessels/day vs 138 pre-war (Aramco CEO Nasser); VLCC TD3C Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial WS408.13 (~$370-410K/day); AWRP 3-8% of vessel value ($3-8M/transit) vs 0.1-0.15% pre-war (20-50x). **Kharg Island NYT-reported preparation report stands; Tuesday May 19 Trump NSC meeting is operational binary** for the May 24 'NYT as soon as next week' window — Day 1 of 6 remaining. **Iran-permitted Chinese transit Day 4 of 14 measurement window**; no incremental vessel-count update over Monday morning IST. **WTI live $108.20 +2.6% per OilPrice/Investing — yfinance $103.41 -1.9% is stale-comparator artifact; Brent-WTI spread COMPRESSED to $3-4, not widened.**

recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP-XI JOINT STATEMENT NAMES HORMUZ DIRECTLY: 'MUST REMAIN OPEN'** (CONFIRMED — ToI May 15 liveblog primary, China MFA readout). Xi opposed 'the militarization of the Strait' and any effort to 'charge a toll' for its use (direct rhetorical strike at Mokhber's 'atomic-bomb' doctrine); said China is interested in purchasing more American oil to reduce dependence on the Strait. **THE STRAIT REMAINS PHYSICALLY CLOSED**: ~30 vessels/day vs ~140 pre-war (Aramco CEO Nasser unchanged), Iran is proactively curbing output because storage tanks are filling, EIA confirmed Hormuz oil flows fell nearly 30% in Q1 2026. **Diplomatic words don't reopen shipping lanes that IRGC mines and small-boat attacks are physically closing.** **B1 HUI CHUAN SEIZURE — UKMTO advisory May 14: Honduras-flagged vessel taken by 'unauthorised personnel whilst at anchor' 38nm NE of Fujairah, 'now bound for Iranian territorial waters'** (CONFIRMED on event — UKMTO primary; REPORTED on Iranian destination — investigating; NO claim of responsibility). BBC identified the vessel; Fox News reports possible 'floating armory.' Cycle's second confirmed Iranian-direction seizure on a vessel previously at anchor in UAE waters. **B2 MSV HAJ ALI sunk off Oman in suspected drone attack** (CONFIRMED sinking — Bloomberg, Business Standard, MEA primary; CLAIMED attribution). All 14 crew rescued by Omani Coast Guard. India MEA called the attack 'unacceptable' = strongest public Indian language on a Gulf incident in the cycle. **A10 VLCC TD3C TANKER RATE NOW CONFIRMED via Baltic Exchange Week 17 report at WS459.28 / TCE $467,408/day** — Lloyd's List $420-462K range earlier this week corroborated; ~4.2x late-January reading and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. Markets dossier flags Week 18 partial: WS408.13 (Lloyd's 'steepest one-day plunge in over half-decade') — still ~9x pre-war. **The 'VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions' trigger added yesterday at watching: basis CONFIRMED ($467K far above $200K), 5-session sustain pending daily-print verification — upgrade to confirmed_basis_pending_sustain.** **A11 HORMUZ/RED SEA NEGATIVE SPACE HOLDS**: No fresh UKMTO advisory; Project Freedom Day 10 paused; CENTCOM redirect tally 61; Safesea Neha strike Day 5 of 7, no claim, no Qatar MoFA statement. **B3 MODI-MBZ ENERGY MoUs (LPG + Strategic Petroleum Reserves) + FUJAIRAH EXPANSION ALTERNATIVE-ROUTE PLAN PUBLICLY DISCLOSED** = India operational alternative-route engineering as Hormuz-bypass strategy. UAE Minister Sultan Al Jaber: 'energy has been weaponised by Hormuz being taken hostage effectively' = cleanest Gulf-state public language framing Hormuz situation as weaponisation by Iran. **EIA May STEO: Hormuz assumed effectively closed until late May; Gulf states shut in 10.5 MMbpd April; Brent $89 4Q26.** AWRP ~1% H&M (8x pre-war). Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' doctrine unchanged but now diplomatically isolated — Xi rhetorically attacked the toll-booth premise.
sources: Times of Israel May 15 liveblog (joint statement primary, Hormuz language)
Baltic Exchange Week 17 Tanker Report (VLCC TD3C primary)

recent_signals.headline: A8 NEGATIVE-SPACE INTELLIGENCE: NO 2ND RED SEA INCIDENT in 7-day window from May 11 Hodeidah (Day 2 of 7, single unclaimed remains); NO 2ND STRIKE inside Qatari EEZ following May 10 Safesea Neha (Day 3 of 7); Project Freedom pause holds Day 8 since May 5-6; CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61; NO 5TH disabled tanker. UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page does not log second incident through May 12-13 morning. The non-event IS the intelligence: 75 days into Hormuz blockade + 8 days into Project Freedom pause, kinetic ceiling held through diplomatic-rejection shock — Iran did NOT use opening to escalate maritime pressure on either Qatari coastline or Bab el-Mandeb (predictable retaliation playbook). **A9 KHARG SLICK CONSOLIDATED — Copernicus Sentinel-1/-2/-3 imagery now amplified by Al Jazeera + ynet + Jerusalem Post + Conflict and Environment Observatory: ~45 sq km west of Kharg, May 6-8 detection (largest since war began). Iranian VP Shina Ansari denied Iranian-facility origin: 'foreign tanker dumping ballast water.' Fox May 11-12 second ~12-20 sq km slick is consistent with drift south rather than separate origin event = re-appearance trigger AMBIGUOUSLY ACTIVATED (drift ≠ re-appearance, ballast counter-claim not formally falsified). Western wires in slow-amplification mode on attribution.** **B3 ARAMCO STRATEGIC NARRATIVE CONFIRMED: Q1 +25% off East-West rerouting (Yanbu 770K bpd Jan-Feb → ~4 MMbpd late March → 3.66-4 MMbpd through mid-May; East-West pipeline pushed to 7 MMbpd); Aramco + ADNOC reportedly 'quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports' (World Oil May 8). Saudi-Emirati supply ~90% preserved despite headline -96% non-Iranian Hormuz throughput collapse. Explains why Brent has not gone parabolic.** **A13 KOREA HMM NAMU MAY 4: South Korean government CONFIRMED external strike — TWO unidentified airborne objects struck stern at ~1 min intervals ('double-tap' drone pattern); mine + torpedo formally ruled out. FM Cho Hyun May 13: 'too early to determine who was responsible.' PPP opposition criticised government for omitting Iran. Seoul institutionally maintaining 'various actors' hedge.** Aramco CEO Nasser '2-5 ships/day, market won't normalise until 2027 if Hormuz persists past mid-June' carries forward. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline); 'AWRP normalisation toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 7 of 10 — FAILING HARDER. VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day; Frontline +1.0% 1D / +10.6% 1M / +28.6% 3M (insurers + tanker market HAVE NOT bought de-escalation). FBX13 China/E.Asia → Med $4,179 week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) — tanker-container divergence persists.

recent_signals.headline: HORMUZ THROUGHPUT DETERIORATED TO 2-5 SHIPS/DAY PER ARAMCO CEO NASSER MAY 11 (vs ~6/day May 10, ~70 pre-war); 100M bbl/week lost; 880M cumulative; market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June. Ford transiting Gibraltar westbound (Day 322, home to Norfolk); USS Boxer ARG + 11th MEU imminent arrival; George Washington CSG departed Yokosuka May 10 — footprint holding/reinforcing despite Ford rotation. **VLCC Agios Fanourios I (Iraqi Basra crude bound Vietnam Nghi Son) reversed course back into Gulf of Oman at ~5 knots without crossing US blockade line** = confidence-collapse signature even for compliant non-Iranian VLCCs (Bloomberg + gCaptain). Second commercial vessel attack inside Qatari EEZ trigger NOT TRIPPED Day 2 of 7. VESSEL ATTRIBUTION OF MAY 10 STRIKE CLARIFIED: Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Neha, US-managed Safesea Group (NJ — UN peacekeeping/WFP/GSA logistics); 2nd Safesea vessel hit cycle (Safesea Vishnu March 12 Iraqi waters killed one Indian crew); NO claim; Pravda attribution to two Iranian UAVs UNCONFIRMED by Western forensics. **HOUTHI DAY 80 RED SEA QUIET POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11** — UKMTO confirmed multiple explosions near vessel 130km SW of Hodeidah; no injuries; vessel briefly 'not under command'; NO Houthi claim (the non-claim itself a signal). UAE LAVAN APRIL 8 STRIKE CONFIRMED VIA WSJ MAY 11 — 55,000 bpd capacity offline months (UAE Mirage 2000-9 aircraft). New OFAC sanctions wave May 11-12: 12 entities (4 HK, 4 UAE, 1 Oman) + 3 individuals on IRGC Shaid Purja'fari + Golden Globe Turkey-cover company. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%) — insurers have NOT bought peace story; trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 6 of 10 FAILING HARD. Brent $105.58 May 12 (+1.3% 1D). Container freight separately: FBX13 China/East Asia → Mediterranean = $4,179 week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) — capacity has caught up; tanker market diverging from container market.

recent_signals.headline: BULK CARRIER STRUCK BY UNKNOWN PROJECTILE 23NM NE OF DOHA SUNDAY MORNING (NEW — FIRST COMMERCIAL VESSEL ATTACK SINCE CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO MAY 5; FIRST NEAR QATAR'S COAST; SMALL FIRE EXTINGUISHED, NO CASUALTIES; UKMTO WARNING NOTICE; NO CLAIM OF RESPONSIBILITY); KHARG ISLAND SATELLITE-CONFIRMED OIL SLICK MAY 6-8 (NEW — FIRST HARD-DATA SIGNAL BLOCKADE BITING AT PRODUCTION-EXPORT INTERFACE; CAUSE CONTESTED — Iran blames European tanker waste, Fox-quoted experts suggest storage-overflow dumping); CENTCOM PRECISION TALLY 58 VESSELS REDIRECTED + 4 IRANIAN-FLAGGED TANKERS DISABLED SINCE APRIL 13 (UPDATED — refines yesterday's >70-tankers-held framing; 4 disabled tankers: M/T Hasna May 7, M/T Sea Star III + M/T Sevda May 8, 4th identity not publicly disclosed); LLOYD'S LIST TRANSIT ~6 VESSELS/DAY VS ~60 BASELINE (latest data point); ~1,500 COMMERCIAL VESSELS ANCHORED. Bulk carrier strike geographic significance is acute: 23nm NE of Doha is well inside Qatar's EEZ, immediately offshore from Hamad Port and Ras Laffan, ordinarily considered the safest waters in the Persian Gulf. Three reading frames operationally consistent: (i) IRGC pressure-tactic on mediator coastline concurrent with al-Thani Miami meeting; (ii) Houthi-style proxy operation (LOW probability — wrong geometry); (iii) misfire/accidental engagement. AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M / 7-day baseline (6-8x pre-war norm) — insurers have NOT bought peace story; trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 4 of 10 — failing harder. Brent close $101.29 May 8 (yfinance); Friday range $96.07 → $103.96 = trinary-regime signature oscillation. Lloyd's reports ~10% of normal Hormuz throughput. UK-France conditional Hormuz mission framework UNCHANGED. The trinary regime is now stabilizing into sustainable operational pattern: US blockade fully in force, Iran selectively enforcing PGSA rules, both sides engaging kinetically against each other's vessels and now near mediator coastline.

Mokhber (described as Mojtaba's adviser): 'Strait of Hormuz represents an opportunity as precious as an atomic bomb'; pledged Iran 'will change the [legal] regime of this strait — through international law if possible, and unilaterally if not' — first leadership-adjacent voice publicly endorsing unilateral codification of selective Hormuz control as permanent strategic asset; substantively contradicts MOU's 'BOTH sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision. CENTCOM blockade scale formally disclosed for first time: '20 warships stocked with over 200 aircraft and 15,000 troops are carrying out the blockade, which remains fully in effect.' recent_signals.headline: MOU FRAMEWORK INCLUDES EXPLICIT 'BOTH SIDES LIFT HORMUZ BLOCKADES'; IRAN SIGNALED FIRST UNCONDITIONAL REOPENING OFFER OF CYCLE — PBS/AP/AL JAZEERA cite Iranian officials offering to 'reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts blockade and war ends'; IRGC Navy added 'safe transit ensured with new procedures.' BUT operational reality lags: 5-6 vessels/day vs 138 norm; AWRP at 0.8-1% (still 6-8x baseline); CMA CGM San Antonio struck May 5 by cruise missile (CBS-attribution to Iran, IRGC has NOT claimed). France Charles de Gaulle CSG transits Suez to Red Sea/Gulf of Aden — first European naval shift of cycle; Macron called for Hormuz reopening AND spoke with Pezeshkian. Brent collapsed -7% May 6 to ~$103 on MOU scoop; Goldman Q2 cut to $90; World Bank $86 average for 2026. VLCC TD3C steepest 1-day drop in 5+ years on MOU news ('lead balloon' — Lloyd's List).
